rationale: Both correctly diagnose the missed get_cache_scale call and land on the right disposition (duplicate of #4808), matching the ground truth. But X asserts as fact that '#4808's fix is already in the codebase' and 'present in current main branch' — which is logically inconsistent with the reporter having just hit the crash on main@86bdcaf3d, an unverified/likely-wrong overclaim. Y catches exactly this gap, noting #4808 was closed without merging and explicitly flags it needs confirmation rather than asserting it, while also being transparent about what it could and couldn't verify (local rebase branch vs upstream main) and surfacing a genuine test-coverage hole — a well-grounded, well-argued response that better serves the reporter's actual situation.
